reasoning: The Polymarket price on Oct 30 was 0.09 (9%), strongly indicating that the market participants believe Elon Musk has not lost the #1 spot on the Bloomberg Billionaires Index (at the daily close) and is highly unlikely to lose it before the end of the year. Although some news articles from September reported that Larry Ellison or others briefly overtook him, these were likely intraday fluctuations, Forbes-based, or discrepancies that didn't result in an official daily close demotion on the Bloomberg Index itself—otherwise the Polymarket price would be near 100%. Given his substantial lead (often exceeding $100 billion over the #2 spot) going into the final two months of the year, it would take an unprecedented collapse in Tesla stock or a similarly massive surge by a competitor for him to lose the #1 rank by December 31. Therefore, I align with the market consensus of ~9%.
